description_html:
About the Event:

Luminary Talks is a optics and photonics presentation competition. The event provides a dynamic platform for students to present innovative ideas, emerging technologies, and impactful applications in the field of optics and photonics.

The competition encourages interdisciplinary participation while maintaining a strong technical focus on light-based technologies. Participants will showcase their conceptual clarity, innovation, and ability to communicate complex optical principles effectively.

Event Structure:

Stage 1: Abstract Submission (Online Round)

  • Teams must submit a one-page abstract in Word format.
  • The abstract must include:
    • Title of the topic
    • Brief description of the concept
    • Optical or photonics principle involved
    • Relevance, innovation, and potential impact
  • Abstracts will be shortlisted based on clarity, originality, and technical relevance.

Abstract Submission Deadline: 21st March 2026

Stage 2: In-Person Presentation (Final Round)

  • Shortlisted teams will deliver a live technical presentation at IIITDM Kancheepuram during the fest.
  • Total Duration (Presentation + Q&A): Maximum 10 minutes
    • Presentation: Maximum 5 minutes
    • Q&A Session: 4 minutes
  • Participants must strictly adhere to the allotted time limits.

Presentation Guidelines:

  • Maximum of 10 slides permitted.
  • Content must clearly relate to optics and/or photonics.
  • All images, figures, and references must be properly cited.
  • If included, demonstration or concept videos must not exceed 30 seconds.

Suggested Topics (Not Limited To):

Participants may choose any topic related to optics and photonics, including:

  • Optics in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Vision
  • Photonics for Sustainable and Smart Systems
  • Biomedical and Healthcare Optics
  • Quantum Optics and Secure Optical Communication
  • Integrated and Silicon Photonics
  • Design-Centric Optical Innovations (AR/VR, perception, illumination)

Original topics beyond the listed domains are permitted, provided they maintain a strong connection to optics or photonics.

Judging Criteria:

Presentations will be evaluated based on:

  • Technical understanding and accuracy
  • Innovation and originality
  • Relevance and potential real-world impact
  • Quality of presentation and communication
  • Effectiveness in handling questions during the Q&A session

The decision of the judges will be final and binding.

General Rules:

  • Plagiarism in any form will result in immediate disqualification.
  • Participants must strictly adhere to time limits and event guidelines.
  • Any form of misconduct may lead to disqualification.
  • The organizing committee reserves the right to modify the event structure or rules if necessary
